stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
The source indicates the model is available for text generation pipelines.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of large language models (LLMs) and text generation.
The associated tags indicate relevance to transformers, safetensors, and the Llama family of models.

stories.title: Cited but Not Verified: Parsing and Evaluating Source Attribution in LLM Deep Research Agents
stories.author: Corey Feld
bullet_points: LLM-powered deep research agents struggle with reliably verifying the sources they cite.
Current methods (self-citation or RAG) fail to validate source accessibility, relevance, or factual consistency.
The paper introduces a novel source attribution evaluation framework using an AST parser to address this critical gap.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes common ML frameworks and formats like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration enables faster performance when running Ollama specifically on Apple Silicon devices.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It utilizes local AI coding agents that run entirely on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is collaborating with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open-source safety tooling.

stories.title: All the latest updates on AI data centers
stories.author: Justine Calma
bullet_points: AI data centers are the critical physical infrastructure supporting the expansion of AI technologies.
The rapid build-out of these energy-intensive facilities is causing global conflicts.
Key concerns surrounding data center expansion include strain on power grids, environmental impact, and community opposition.

stories.title: Course correction: Google to link more sources in AI Overviews
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is enhancing its AI Overviews feature to improve source attribution.
The update involves linking to more external sources within the AI-generated summaries.
This change aims to increase transparency and verifiability of information provided by Google's AI.

stories.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
stories.author: Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
bullet_points: ActCam is a zero-shot method for video generation that allows fine-grained control over both character motion and camera trajectory.
It jointly transfers character motion from a source video into a new scene while enabling per-frame control of camera parameters (intrinsic and extrinsic).
The model is designed to build upon existing pretrained image-to-video diffusion models conditioned on scene depth.

stories.title: AI Co-Mathematician: Accelerating Mathematicians with Agentic AI
stories.author: Pushmeet Kohli
bullet_points: Introduction of an 'AI co-mathematician,' a workbench designed to assist mathematicians in open-ended research.
The system provides holistic support across the entire mathematical workflow, from ideation and literature search to theorem proving.
Key features include an asynchronous, stateful workspace capable of managing uncertainty and tracking failed hypotheses.

stories.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
stories.author: Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
bullet_points: ActCam is a zero-shot method designed for high-fidelity video generation, addressing the need for fine-grained control over both character motion and camera cinematography.
It jointly transfers character motion from a source video into a new scene while allowing per-frame control over intrinsic and extrinsic camera parameters.
The model is built upon existing pretrained image-to-video diffusion models, conditioning them using scene depth and other inputs.

stories.title: Why Global LLM Leaderboards Are Misleading: Small Portfolios for Heterogeneous Supervised ML
stories.author: Swati Gupta
bullet_points: The study challenges the validity of current global LLM leaderboards, which rely on pairwise human feedback.
Analysis of 89K comparisons across 52 LLMs shows that the best-fit Bradley-Terry ranking is misleading.
The top 50 models ranked by the global BT metric are statistically indistinguishable, with win probabilities barely exceeding 0.53.

stories.title: CPanel's Black Week: 3 New Vulnerabilities Patched After Attack on 44k Servers
stories.author: ggallas
bullet_points: CPanel experienced a security incident resulting in attacks on 44,000 servers.
The incident led to the discovery and patching of three new vulnerabilities.
This highlights ongoing critical security risks within widely used web hosting infrastructure.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating a focus on generative AI tasks.
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance to modern deep learning frameworks.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is expanding its capabilities to support local image generation.
The feature is currently available for macOS users in an experimental capacity.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Amazon is adding a vertical video feed to Prime Video
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Amazon Prime Video is integrating a vertical video feed, similar to TikTok, into its platform.
The new 'Clips' feed will feature short-form video content derived from existing shows and movies.
Users can transition from the short clips to the full title, which they can then rent or purchase.

stories.title: Here is Yarbo’s promise to fix the robot mower that ran me over
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: A robot lawn mower from Yarbo was successfully hacked, leading to a dangerous incident.
The vulnerability allows hackers to access sensitive personal data, including GPS coordinates and Wi-Fi passwords.
Yarbo has issued a statement promising a fix for the security flaw in their devices.

stories.title: The new Wild West of AI kids’ toys
stories.author: Sophie Charara, WIRED.com
bullet_points: New connected AI companions are emerging as children's toys.
These toys have the potential to significantly change children's play and routines.
The technology is raising concerns, leading some lawmakers to call for bans.

stories.title: Manufacturing qubits that can move
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: The article discusses the technical challenge of integrating electronic manufacturing with flexible qubit geometries.
This points to ongoing hurdles in scaling and developing advanced quantum computing hardware.
The focus is on the intersection of materials science and quantum engineering.

stories.title: Chaos erupts as cyberattack disrupts learning platform Canvas amid finals
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A cyberattack disrupted the widely used learning platform, Canvas.
The incident caused widespread disruption across schools and colleges nationwide.
As a result of the attack, year-end tests and exams were postponed.

stories.title: Elon Musk faces criminal probe in France after ignoring summons in X case
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Elon Musk is facing a criminal probe in France.
The probe stems from an issue related to the X platform.
France is threatening criminal charges if Musk fails to appear for questioning.

stories.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: Introduces UniPool, a novel approach to Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures.
Challenges the conventional design where each transformer layer has its own isolated expert set.
Suggests that uniform random routing can maintain high accuracy when replacing learned top-k routers in deeper layers.

stories.title: BAMI: Training-Free Bias Mitigation in GUI Grounding
stories.author: Jiwen Lu
bullet_points: The research addresses limitations in GUI grounding, a key capability for autonomous GUI agents.
The study identifies two primary sources of error in complex GUI tasks: precision bias (due to high image resolution) and ambiguity bias (due to intricate elements).
A novel method, Masked Prediction Distribution (MPD) attribution, is proposed for training-free bias mitigation.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in LLMs: their inability to generate novel, valid, and challenging mathematical problems.
Introduces VHG, a new method that uses verification to ensure the generated problems are mathematically sound.
Offers an alternative to costly human expert involvement or flawed self-play methods for advancing LLM training.

stories.title: Optimizer-Model Consistency: Full Finetuning with the Same Optimizer as Pretraining Forgets Less
stories.author: Tong Zhang
bullet_points: The research proposes that using the same optimizer during finetuning as was used during pretraining for LLMs improves the learning-forgetting tradeoff.
This 'optimizer-model consistency' approach reportedly achieves better performance on new tasks while minimizing knowledge loss compared to other optimizers and LoRA.
The findings offer a novel methodological insight into optimizing the supervised finetuning (SFT) stage of large language models.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established ground-truth benchmarks exist for a specific context.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' to formalize this evaluation setting.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
stories.author: Anshumali Shrivastava
bullet_points: Current retrieval-augmented agents often treat information retrieval as a 'black box' process.
Existing methods mimic novice searching by iteratively refining queries based on retrieved snippets.
The proposed advancement aims to move beyond this by incorporating expert-level priors for more efficient knowledge navigation.

stories.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
stories.author: Olga Fink
bullet_points: The study addresses the ambiguity in Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG) progress, questioning if reported gains are genuine or due to inconsistent evaluation.
Current MMDG research is noted as being fragmented, with significant variations across datasets and experimental setups.
Existing benchmarks are criticized for focusing too heavily on action recognition while neglecting other critical real-world applications.

stories.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
stories.author: Zhenfei Yin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of optimizing LLM agents for long-horizon decision-making, which is currently limited by purely reactive methods.
Introduces StraTA (Strategic Trajectory Abstraction), a framework that integrates an explicit trajectory-level strategy into agentic Reinforcement Learning (RL).
Aims to improve exploration and credit assignment over extended decision-making trajectories in LLM-based agents.

stories.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: Introduces UniPool, a novel approach to Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures.
Challenges the conventional MoE design where each transformer layer has isolated expert capacity.
Suggests that replacing layer-specific routers with uniform random routing has a minimal impact on downstream accuracy.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: LLMs struggle to generate high-quality, novel mathematical problems necessary for advanced training and autonomous research.
The paper introduces VHG, a new method designed to generate hard, verifiable problems for LLMs.
VHG aims to overcome limitations of existing methods, which rely on expensive human experts or suffer from reward hacking in self-play.

stories.title: Optimizer-Model Consistency: Full Finetuning with the Same Optimizer as Pretraining Forgets Less
stories.author: Tong Zhang
bullet_points: The research investigates the role of optimizers in Large Language Model (LLM) training, specifically comparing pretraining and finetuning.
The study observes that using the exact same optimizer during full finetuning as was used during pretraining leads to a better learning-forgetting tradeoff.
This approach reportedly outperforms other optimizers and even LoRA during the Supervised Finetuning (SFT) stage.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established benchmark or ground-truth labels exist for a specific domain.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' to formalize this evaluation setting.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can be considered valid deployment evidence.

stories.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
stories.author: Anshumali Shrivastava
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in current Retrieval-Augmented Agents (RAGs) which often treat retrieval as a 'black box'.
Existing methods mimic novice searching by iteratively refining queries based on snippets, leading to inefficiency.
The proposed 'Superintelligent Retrieval Agent' aims to incorporate expert-level priors for more efficient knowledge base navigation.

stories.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
stories.author: Olga Fink
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of clarity regarding genuine progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG).
Current MMDG research suffers from fragmentation due to inconsistent evaluation protocols across studies.
Existing benchmarks are criticized for focusing too heavily on action recognition while neglecting broader real-world applications.

stories.title: EMO: Pretraining Mixture of Experts for Emergent Modularity
stories.author: Sewon Min
bullet_points: The paper addresses the inefficiency of monolithic Large Language Models (LLMs) by exploring Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures.
A key challenge identified is that restricting inference to a subset of experts often causes severe performance degradation in practice.
The research proposes 'EMO' (Pretraining Mixture of Experts) as a solution to enable practical, modular LLM deployment.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: LLMs excel at solving problems but struggle to generate novel, valid, and challenging problems for training.
The paper introduces VHG, a method that uses verification to generate high-quality mathematical reasoning problems.
VHG aims to overcome limitations of current methods, which rely on expensive human experts or prone-to-error self-play.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of evaluating LLM safety when no pre-existing labeled benchmarks are available.
Proposes a framework for 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' using scenario-based audits.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which such audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
stories.author: Zhenfei Yin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of long-horizon decision-making in LLM-based agents, where current methods are too reactive.
Introduces StraTA (Strategic Trajectory Abstraction), a framework to incorporate explicit trajectory-level strategies into agentic RL.
Aims to improve exploration and credit assignment over extended decision-making sequences.

stories.title: Recursive Agent Optimization
stories.author: Graham Neubig
bullet_points: Introduces Recursive Agent Optimization (RAO), a new reinforcement learning approach.
RAO trains agents capable of recursively spawning and delegating sub-tasks to themselves.
This recursive structure enables agents to scale to longer contexts and tackle complex problems using divide-and-conquer.

stories.title: Can RL Teach Long-Horizon Reasoning to LLMs? Expressiveness Is Key
stories.author: Abulhair Saparov
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in current LLM reasoning research: the lack of controlled, scalable environments for studying training difficulty.
Introduces 'ScaleLogic,' a synthetic framework designed to independently control two critical axes of reasoning difficulty: proof planning depth (horizon) and logical expressiveness.
Investigates whether Reinforcement Learning (RL) can effectively teach long-horizon reasoning capabilities to Large Language Models (LLMs).

stories.title: MASPO: Joint Prompt Optimization for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Min Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MASPO, a novel framework for optimizing prompts in LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems (MAS).
Addresses the challenge of aligning local agent objectives with overall system goals in collaborative tasks.
Focuses on the difficulty of jointly optimizing role-specific prompts across interacting agents.

stories.title: Anthropic hit B ARR in 16 months. I went looking for where the money is actually coming from.
stories.author: thegdsks
bullet_points: The article discusses Anthropic reaching $1B in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) in 16 months.
The author is investigating the actual sources of Anthropic's revenue, suggesting a deeper dive beyond simple growth metrics.
The piece critiques the superficiality of revenue charts, emphasizing the need to understand 'who pays' versus 'who gets charged'.

stories.title: How I Built a Masking Tool Without Showing AI Any Real Data: Column-wise Shuffling as the Scaffold
stories.author: _d3709cf9e80fc6babbff
bullet_points: The article details a method for sanitizing sensitive business data before using it with Large Language Models (LLMs).
The core technique involves 'column-wise shuffling' combined with the Faker library to create LLM-safe samples.
A notable aspect is the development process, which was completed without the author writing any Python code.

stories.title: agentic terminals are eating the IDE (and why that is not entirely good)
stories.author: pvgomes
bullet_points: The article discusses the growing trend of 'agentic terminals' replacing traditional Integrated Development Environments (IDEs) for senior engineers.
It projects this shift to become the default workflow by 2026.
The piece also raises concerns regarding the governance and potential downsides associated with this new paradigm.
